title: Under-Desk Cable Management Tray
summary: A practical look at whether cable trays solve a real daily problem or just hide clutter.
verdict: Worth it for fixed desks with a monitor, dock, and charger. Skip for minimalist laptop-only setups.
points: Useful when your desk has a back lip or pre-drilled mounting points.
The win is fewer dangling cables and fewer accidental pulls.
Skip it if you rent with strict no-screw rules or only use two cables.

title: USB-C Hub Checklist
summary: The three specs that matter more than the product photo.
verdict: Buy known-brand hubs with clear specs. Skip listings with vague “4K support” language.
points: Check power delivery wattage.
Confirm HDMI 4K at 60Hz, not only 30Hz.
Avoid unknown-brand hubs for permanent desk power.

title: Laptop Stand vs Monitor Arm
summary: A no-hype comparison for people building a workday setup.
verdict: Laptop stands fit portable setups; monitor arms fit permanent workstations.
points: Laptop stands are cheap and portable.
Monitor arms are better for fixed daily work.
Neither fixes posture alone; the full setup matters.

title: Desk Lamp Buying Notes
summary: What to check before buying a viral desk lamp.
verdict: Buy for controllable task lighting. Skip lamps that cannot angle away from your eyes.
points: Adjustable height/angle beats RGB features.
Glare control matters more than max brightness.
Avoid unproven health claims.

title: Monitor Arm Red Flags
summary: The recurring complaints that matter before mounting a monitor.
verdict: Buy only after checking weight, VESA, and desk edge.
points: Check monitor weight and VESA compatibility.
Cheap arms sag with ultrawide/heavy displays.
Clamp depth matters on thick or glass desks.

title: Portable Monitor for Work Travel
summary: Who benefits from a second screen and who should skip it.
verdict: Worth it for multi-window work while travelling. Skip if you mostly answer email.
points: Helpful for charting, spreadsheets, coding, and reference docs.
Adds cable clutter and battery drain.
Brightness and stand quality beat resolution.

title: Lavalier Microphone Audio Test
summary: A review framework for compact creator microphones.
verdict: Buy if speech clarity improves in your actual recording setup.
points: Compare raw voice before and after noise reduction.
Check phone compatibility.
Watch for wind noise and clothing rub.

title: MagSafe Desk Stand
summary: A small desk accessory that is only useful in the right setup.
verdict: Buy for visible phone workflows. Skip if it encourages distraction.
points: Useful for calls, timers, and 2FA.
Charging speed and magnet strength vary.
It is clutter if your phone should stay away from the desk.

title: Walking Pad Before You Buy
summary: Noise, space, and returns before buying a desk walking pad.
verdict: Wait until you verify space, noise tolerance, and return policy.
points: Measure storage space first.
Noise matters in apartments and shared offices.
Return shipping can be painful because units are heavy.

title: Desk Mat: Upgrade or Aesthetic?
summary: When a desk mat improves the work surface and when it is just decoration.
verdict: Buy if it solves mouse/keyboard surface issues. Skip if you only want a prettier photo.
points: Helps mouse movement and keyboard sound.
Large mats can trap dust.
Material smell and edge curling are common complaints.
